NCT01131676.name: EMPA-REG OUTCOME
NCT01131676.group: T2D + established CVD, empagliflozin 10/25 mg vs placebo (3P-MACE HR)

allOutcomes.title: CV death, non-fatal MI, non-fatal stroke (3P-MACE, primary, HR)

NCT01131676.snippet: Source: Zinman B et al. NEJM 2015;373:2117-2128 (EMPA-REG OUTCOME).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 7,020 adults with type 2 diabetes and established cardiovascular disease randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to empagliflozin 10 mg, 25 mg, or matched placebo on top of standard care. Median follow-up 3.1 years.

evidence.label: Primary - 3P-MACE
evidence.text: Composite of CV death, non-fatal MI, non-fatal stroke: empagliflozin 490/4687 (10.5%) vs placebo 282/2333 (12.1%); hazard ratio 0.86 (95.02% CI 0.74-0.99; P=0.04 for superiority). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - CV Death, HF Hospitalisation, All-Cause Mortality
evidence.text: CV death HR 0.62 (0.49-0.77). HF hospitalisation HR 0.65 (0.50-0.85). ACM HR 0.68 (0.57-0.82). Nominal reductions in non-fatal MI and non-fatal stroke. Safety: genital mycotic infections increased; DKA rare; no disproportionate amputations or fractures.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 97% vital status. D4 LOW - centrally adjudicated endpoints. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~1. Diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus prior to informed consent~2. Male or female patients on diet and exercise regimen who are drug naive or pre treated with any background therapy. Antidiabetic therapy has to be unchanged for 12 weeks prior to randomization.~3. Glycosylated haemoglobin (HbA1c) of >= 7.0% and <=10% for patients on background therapy or HbA1c >= 7.0% and <= 9.0% for drug naive patients~4. Age >= 18 years~5. Body Mass index <= 45 at Visit 1~6. Signed...

NCT01032629.name: CANVAS
NCT01032629.group: T2D + high CV risk, canagliflozin 100/300 mg vs placebo (3P-MACE HR, CANVAS Program integrated)

allOutcomes.title: CV death, non-fatal MI, non-fatal stroke (primary, HR, CANVAS Program)

NCT01032629.snippet: Source: Neal B et al. NEJM 2017;377:644-657 (CANVAS Program integrated analysis).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 10,142 adults with T2D and high CV risk (66% with CVD, 34% primary prevention with >=2 risk factors) randomized double-blind across two integrated trials (CANVAS + CANVAS-R) to canagliflozin 100/300 mg or placebo. Mean follow-up 2.4-3.6 years.

evidence.label: Primary - 3P-MACE (Integrated)
evidence.text: Composite 3P-MACE event rate: canagliflozin 26.9 vs placebo 31.5 per 1000 patient-years; integrated hazard ratio 0.86 (95% CI 0.75-0.97; P<0.001 non-inferiority, P=0.02 superiority). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - HF Hosp, Renal, Lower-Limb Amputation
evidence.text: HF hospitalisation HR 0.67 (0.52-0.87). Renal composite (40% eGFR fall, RRT, renal death) HR 0.60 (0.47-0.77). Lower-limb amputation HR 1.97 (1.41-2.75) - P<0.001 harmful signal (attenuated in later CREDENCE trial). Fracture HR 1.26 (NS after sensitivity). Mycotic infections increased.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 99% vital status. D4 LOW - centrally adjudicated. D5 SOME CONCERNS - CANVAS and CANVAS-R used different protocol versions integrated post-hoc per pre-specified SAP; amputation signal flagged post-publication.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 30 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Patients must have a diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus and greater than or equal to (>=) 30 yrs old with history of cardiovascular (CV) event, or >= 50 yrs old with high risk of CV events~* Patients must have inadequate diabetes control (as defined by glycosylated hemoglobin greater than or equal to 7.0% to less than or equal to 10.5% at screening) and be either (1) not currently on diabetes drug therapy or (2) on therapy with any approved class of diabetes drugs~Exc...

NCT01730534.name: DECLARE-TIMI 58
NCT01730534.group: T2D + CVD/high-risk, dapagliflozin 10 mg vs placebo (3P-MACE HR - co-primary)
NCT01730534.snippet: Source: Wiviott SD et al. NEJM 2019;380:347-357 (DECLARE-TIMI 58).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 17,160 adults with T2D and either established atherosclerotic CVD (41%) or multiple risk factors (59%) randomized 1:1 double-blind to dapagliflozin 10 mg or matched placebo. Median follow-up 4.2 years.

evidence.label: Co-Primary - 3P-MACE and CV-Death/HF-Hosp
evidence.text: 3P-MACE: dapagliflozin 756/8582 (8.8%) vs placebo 803/8578 (9.4%); HR 0.93 (95% CI 0.84-1.03; P=0.17 superiority). Non-inferiority met but superiority not. Co-primary CV-death/HF-hosp: HR 0.83 (0.73-0.95; P=0.005).

evidence.label: Secondary - HF Hospitalisation, Renal, Primary Prevention
evidence.text: HF hospitalisation alone: HR 0.73 (0.61-0.88). Renal composite HR 0.76 (0.67-0.87). 3P-MACE benefit driven by secondary-prevention subgroup (established CVD HR 0.90, 0.79-1.02) with minimal benefit in primary prevention. DKA signal: 0.3% dapa vs 0.1% placebo (HR 2.18).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 99% vital status. D4 LOW - centrally adjudicated. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; max age 130 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Provision of informed consent prior to any study specific procedures~* Female or male aged ≥40 years~* Diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes~* High Risk for Cardiovascular events~Exclusion Criteria:~* Diagnosis of Type 1 diabetes mellitus History of bladder cancer or history of radiation therapy to the lower abdomen or pelvis at any time~* Chronic cystitis and/or recurrent urinary tract infections~* Pregnant or breast-feeding patients"

NCT01986881.name: VERTIS-CV
NCT01986881.group: T2D + established ASCVD, ertugliflozin 5/15 mg vs placebo (3P-MACE HR - non-inferiority)

allOutcomes.title: 3P-MACE (primary non-inferiority met, superiority not tested formally, HR)

NCT01986881.snippet: Source: Cannon CP et al. NEJM 2020;383:1425-1435 (VERTIS-CV).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 8,246 adults with T2D and established ASCVD randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to ertugliflozin 5 mg, ertugliflozin 15 mg, or matched placebo. Mean follow-up 3.5 years.

evidence.label: Primary - 3P-MACE Non-Inferiority
evidence.text: 3P-MACE: ertugliflozin pooled 653/5493 (11.9%) vs placebo 327/2747 (11.9%); HR 0.97 (95.6% CI 0.85-1.11; P<0.001 non-inferiority). Non-inferiority primary met; superiority tested hierarchically and not significant.

evidence.label: Secondary - HF Hospitalisation and Renal
evidence.text: HF hospitalisation alone: HR 0.70 (0.54-0.90) - consistent with class effect despite null 3P-MACE. Renal composite: HR 0.81 (0.63-1.04). No unique amputation or fracture signal.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 98% vital status. D4 LOW - centrally adjudicated. D5 LOW - SAP locked, hierarchical testing pre-specified.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria (Overall Cardiovascular Study):~* Diagnosis of T2DM in accordance with American Diabetes Association (ADA) guidelines~* Hemoglobin A1c (A1C) at the start of study participation of 7.0-10.5% (53-91 mmol/mol)~* On stable allowable anti-hyperglycemic agents (AHA) or on no background AHA for at least 8 weeks prior to the study participation~* Body Mass Index (BMI) > or = to 18.0 kg/m^2~* Evidence or a history of atherosclerosis involving the coronary, cerebral or peripheral vas...
